source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/09_HnVUE/92_인증 - FDA/참고샘플 - Aspenview/03.[AI-AspenView-SDS] Software design spectification_Life Cycle.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView software design specification content describing SRS, source code, Operation Manual traceability and a Waterfall Life Cycle Model.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to document traceability between SRS, source code, and Operation Manual, and to describe software development life cycle phases.
keyPoints: The document purpose is to describe relations between SRS, source code, and Operation Manual.
The software development process follows a classic Waterfall Life Cycle Model.
Qualification verifies that implemented design and code meet original requirements.
After release, software changes are limited to identified defects or approved enhancements.

entities.name: System Discrepancy Reports (SDR)
entities.evidence: All identified system software defects are recorded on System Discrepancy Reports (SDR) forms

entities.name: Modification Request (MR) database
entities.evidence: or submitted electronically to the Modification Request (MR) database

concepts.name: Software requirements specification traceability
concepts.evidence: Purpose of this document is to describe relations between SRS, source code and Operation Manual.

concepts.name: Waterfall Life Cycle Model
concepts.evidence: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

concepts.name: Software qualification
concepts.evidence: The qualification phase verifies that the design and code implemented meet the original requirements.

concepts.name: Software maintenance
concepts.evidence: Once the software is released, the only software changes allowed are to fix Identified defects in the code or to support approved enhancements.

concepts.name: System Discrepancy Report
concepts.evidence: All identified system software defects are recorded on System Discrepancy Reports (SDR) forms

concepts.name: Modification Request
concepts.evidence: or submitted electronically to the Modification Request (MR) database

evidence.claim: The document describes relations between SRS, source code, and Operation Manual for traceability.
evidence.evidenceText: Purpose of this document is to describe relations between SRS, source code and Operation Manual. This document enumerates (enlists, formalizes) items of SRS and Operation Manual for traceability.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows the Waterfall Life Cycle Model.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

evidence.claim: The analysis activity defines and analyzes the software development scope and establishes requirements and work scope before design.
evidence.evidenceText: The scope of software development is defined and analyzed. The activity consists of establishing requirements and work scope before the design phase.

evidence.claim: The planning activity outlines tasks, responsibilities, resources, and other aspects for the development project.
evidence.evidenceText: The tasks, responsibilities, resources, and other aspects pertinent to the specific development project are outlined.

evidence.claim: The design phase addresses data structure, software architecture, functionality, and interface characterization.
evidence.evidenceText: The design phase addresses four functional attributes of the software: data structure, software architecture, functionality, and interface characterization.

evidence.claim: The implementation phase creates software source code in accordance with coding conventions.
evidence.evidenceText: The software source code is created in accordance with coding conventions.

evidence.claim: Design reviews or inspections verify code consistency with company standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Design reviews/inspections are held to verify the consistency of the code created with respect to the company standards.

evidence.claim: The qualification phase verifies that the design and code meet original requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: The qualification phase verifies that the design and code implemented meet the original requirements.

evidence.claim: Testing ensures defined inputs produce intended results that coincide with required outputs.
evidence.evidenceText: Testing ensures that defined inputs will produce intended results that coincide with required outputs.

evidence.claim: After software release, changes are limited to fixing identified defects or supporting approved enhancements.
evidence.evidenceText: Once the software is released, the only software changes allowed are to fix Identified defects in the code or to support approved enhancements.

evidence.claim: Identified system software defects are recorded on SDR forms or submitted to the MR database.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified system software defects are recorded on System Discrepancy Reports (SDR) forms or submitted electronically to the Modification Request (MR) database

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal indicates FDA in source metadata/path, but the extracted source text does not identify FDA as the issuing authority or jurisdiction.
No named regulatory standards are present in the provided source text.
The provided text appears to include pages 3/29 and 4/29 only; complete document metadata may exist outside the excerpt.
